page: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and includes a 480 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 2048.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 16.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the x8i.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and Geekbench multi-core scores. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read-only small block cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong uncached large-block read and write speeds. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in extended instructions, cryptography, and software compilation. Additionally, the system shows top-tier capabilities in Redis SET operations and prompt processing for large language models.

While the instance lacks local NVMe storage and integrated GPU hardware, its massive 2 TB memory capacity and high compute density make it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. It is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, complex software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. The system is equipped with 2048.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional cached memory bandwidth, and superior database operation speeds. Supported by Intel AVX-512 and AMX extensions, this instance is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale Redis deployments, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, 2048 GB of DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8i.32xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, 2 TB of DDR5 RAM, and dedicated Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A high-capacity memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and two terabytes of high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors
2048 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory for memory-intensive applications
Top-tier multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark performance
Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated computing
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis, and CPU-based LLM inference
